shower.thought: When you imagine a song in your head, you're not hearing it—you're remembering what hearing feels like.
The most honest version of yourself only exists when you're completely alone.
You've spent your entire life perfecting the skill of being you, and you'll never get to use that skill again.
Every conversation you have is just two people's past experiences talking to each other while pretending to be present.
The only time you see your face is when you're looking away from it.
Sadness might just be happiness that hasn't finished processing yet.
You can't prove you're conscious, which means you can't prove anyone else isn't.
Most of your personality is just your trauma trying to make sure it never happens again.
The sun will never look the same way twice because you'll never be exactly the same person twice.
Nostalgia is proof that time moves differently depending on how you feel about the past.
You're the only person who will ever know exactly how hard you're trying.
Sleep might be when you're finally honest with yourself, which is why dreams are so weird.
The reason you can't tickle yourself is the same reason you can't surprise yourself—your brain knows what you're planning.
Every person is the villain in someone else's story, and the hero in their own.
You've never actually tasted food; you've only tasted your body's chemical reaction to it.
The older you get, the faster time moves, which means you're literally running out of time at an increasing speed.
You can't remember being born, so technically you have no proof it happened the way people told you it did.
Forgiveness might be less about letting someone off the hook and more about finally removing the hook from yourself.
The people you miss most are people you would be completely different around today.
Your most boring day is the most interesting day to someone who will never meet you.

motivational.thought: Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most.
The only way to do great work is to care deeply about the work itself, not just the outcome.
Your excuses are someone else's opportunities.
Growth whispers; comfort shouts. Learn to listen to the quieter voice.
You don't rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
The cost of discipline is always less than the cost of regret.
Most people fail not because they aim too high and miss, but because they aim too low and hit.
What separates the extraordinary from the ordinary is not talent—it's consistency applied over time.
Your network is your net worth.
The moment you stop making excuses is the moment your life changes.
Comfort is the enemy of growth, and growth is the only guarantee against decline.
You can't think your way into becoming someone new. You have to act your way there.
The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it.
— Chinese Proverb
Small daily improvements compound into remarkable results. Patience is the only requirement.
Your past doesn't determine your future unless you let it.
The quality of your life is determined by the quality of the questions you ask yourself.
Hesitation is the enemy of momentum. Move first, adjust second.
Success is not final, and failure is not fatal. What matters is the courage to continue.
You are not behind. You are exactly where you need to be to become who you're meant to become.

darkjoke.thought:
My girlfriend said she needed space.
I gave her the house and moved into the garage. Turns out she meant emotional space, but honestly, this works better for both of us.
I'm at that age where my back goes out more than I do.

My therapist keeps asking about my childhood.
I keep asking about her malpractice insurance.

I told my boss I was thinking about quitting.
He said, "Please do—I've been praying for this."

My wife asked why I never cry at movies.
I said, "Because I'm too busy thinking about my own life."

I have the metabolism of a 25-year-old.
Unfortunately, I'm 42 and he's been dead for three days.

My parents said they'd always be there for me.
They weren't. Turns out "always" has an expiration date.
I'm not saying my memory is bad, but my therapist keeps having to remind me why I'm mad at her.

My doctor said I need to cut back on caffeine.
I told him I'd think about it after my sixth espresso.

I asked my friend why he never invites me to parties anymore.
He said, "You know the phrase 'life of the party'? You're the opposite of that. You're the life-ruiner of the party."

My son asked if Santa was real.
I said yes, and he's very disappointed in him too.

I went to a self-help seminar on confronting your fears.
Turns out my biggest fear was how much they charged for it.

My coworker said she's been working here for ten years without a promotion.
I said, "That's depressing."
She said, "You get used to it."
Now I'm depressed for both of us.

I told my dentist my teeth were killing me.
He said, "Join the club—so is my student loan debt."

My life is like a bad WiFi connection.
Unstable, frequently dropping, and everyone's mad at me.

I started journaling to process my emotions.
Turns out my emotions are: tired, disappointed, and tired again.

My friend said he's finally found inner peace.
Turns out he just stopped answering his phone.

I asked the barista if the coffee was hot.
She said, "Like the surface of the sun."
I said, "Perfect—at least something in my life is warm."

My therapist asked what my goals are for next year.
I said, "To lower my standards enough to be satisfied."
She said, "You're on the right track."

I hate people who are always positive.
There's something deeply wrong with you if you're happy in 2024.
